what_it_does: Defines a React-based workshop canvas for building, editing, previewing, prototyping, AI-drafting, refining, branching, and exporting multi-step ConceptV wizard flows.
observations: Defines built-in wizard kinds for property, onboarding, and generic flows with shape, description, steps, fields, and choices.
Supports three layouts: sidebar, progress, and cards.
Maintains separate editor state for selected step and prototype state for interactive preview navigation.
Uses global browser APIs and global app services including window.claude, window.WS_AI, window.dsaToast, window.CV_TYPES_SAVE, window.CV_TYPES_PROMPT, CanvasHeader, ViShape, and WSCandidateGallery.
Includes Vi-assisted full-wizard drafting, candidate generation, step alternates, and per-step copy/schema refinement.
Exports generated JSX as clipboard text, downloaded file, or print-to-PDF.
Stores wizard definitions in plain object structures with step kind, title, body, fields, options, and optional choice branching.
standouts: The file combines data presets, editor UI, live renderer, AI prompt orchestration, branching prototype behavior, and JSX export in one module.
Wizard kinds are associated with geometric glyphs and ConceptV-specific naming rules.
Prototype mode preserves field and choice state across steps and supports choice-based jump rules.
conventions: Uses globally available React instead of ES module imports for the live builder file.
Aliases React hooks with a _wz suffix.
Uses nested helper functions inside components for localized state updates.
Persists document edits by spreading the existing doc and patching changed fields through saveDoc.
Represents selectable UI options as arrays of strings and field schemas as small plain objects.
Uses CSS class names with ws-wz, ws-wb, dsa, and cv prefixes, plus some inline styles for compact controls.
concerns: Several helpers assume step.fields or step.options exist before map/filter calls, while other code treats those properties as optional.
The builder relies on many window-level globals, so missing host services would fail at runtime without local guards in some paths.
Choice branching step targets are populated from window.WS_AI bridge active steps instead of directly from the current doc steps.
The non-prototype continue button is enabled even though mock mode renders activeIndex from the editor selection, so the click path does not visibly advance the mock preview.
The exported JSX renderer covers fields and choice options but does not reproduce all in-app renderer states such as capture dropzone, review summary, celebration UI, layouts, or branching.
notes: This file is a self-contained wizard studio surface that mixes schema presets, editing controls, AI generation hooks, interactive preview, and export output.
questions: Should custom wizard kinds from window.WIZARD_KINDS also be used by blankWizard and viDraftWizard, or only by the picker UI?
Should exported JSX preserve layout, branching, and special step renderers from the in-app preview?
Should mock mode navigation be static by design, or should the visible preview advance when Continue is clicked?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is a multi-step flow builder that can act as a visual mock or a state-driven prototype.
apparent_themes: ConceptV brand vocabulary embedded in tooling
intention_signals: The file appears intended to let users quickly assemble ConceptV-branded multi-step flows, use Vi to generate or revise them, test basic interaction paths, and hand off a JSX version.
novelty: The distinct element is the coupling of wizard schema editing, geometric brand glyphs, Vi candidate generation, prototype branching, and export generation inside one workshop canvas.
contribution: Contributes a file-local model for editable wizard substrates: predefined flow archetypes, step schemas, layout variants, AI drafting/refinement hooks, prototype navigation/state handling, choice branching, shape-based visual identity, and JSX export mechanics.
summary_for_embedding: A React workshop canvas for creating ConceptV multi-step wizards with presets, editable steps and fields, AI drafting/refinement, prototype playback, branching choices, layout previews, and JSX export.
intention_for_embedding: This was for turning briefs or templates into editable, previewable, and exportable ConceptV wizard flows inside the Workshop canvas.

symbols.does: Creates a new wizard document scaffold from a wizard kind id.
symbols.description: Looks up a matching wizard kind, falls back to the property kind, and returns a sidebar mock wizard with cloned preset steps. Each generated step receives a randomized id.

symbols.does: Asks Vi to draft a wizard schema from a brief and optional kind constraint.
symbols.description: Builds a structured prompt describing brand voice, known wizard kinds, step kinds, and JSON output rules. It calls window.claude.complete, parses JSON from the reply, normalizes kind and step data, caps steps at eight, and returns a mock sidebar wizard object.

symbols.does: Tests whether a kind value should be treated as automatic selection.
symbols.description: Returns true when the value is missing or equals "auto". viDraftWizard uses it to decide whether the prompt should lock the wizard kind or allow selection from the brief.

symbols.does: Renders the full wizard builder canvas and coordinates editing, preview, AI candidates, type promotion, templates, and export.
symbols.description: Owns UI state for selected step, export menu, prototype position/state, and candidate gallery. It displays document metadata, kind and layout controls, a step strip, the wizard renderer, a step editor, and the candidate gallery.

symbols.does: Patches the current wizard document and saves it.
symbols.description: Wraps saveDoc with a shallow merge of the current doc and a patch object. It is the local write path for layout, mode, title, and step mutations inside WizardBuilder.

symbols.does: Opens the Vi candidate gallery and populates it with generated candidates.
symbols.description: Sets gallery state to busy, requests three candidates from window.WS_AI.generateCandidates for a target, and stores the result. On failure it closes the gallery and shows a toast.

symbols.does: Regenerates gallery candidates using a refinement hint.
symbols.description: Uses the current gallery target and passes the hint as a brief to window.WS_AI.generateCandidates. It toggles the gallery busy state while preserving the open gallery context.

symbols.does: Applies a selected Vi candidate to the document or custom pick handler.
symbols.description: If the gallery supplied an override callback, it delegates to that callback. Otherwise it applies a candidate diff through window.WS_AI.applyDiff, saves the result, closes the gallery, and shows an applied toast.

symbols.does: Switches the wizard kind after user confirmation.
symbols.description: Prompts before replacing the existing steps with the defaults from blankWizard for the selected kind. It preserves the rest of the document, updates wizardKind and steps, and resets the active step to the first step.

symbols.does: Applies a patch to one step by index.
symbols.description: Maps over the current steps and shallow-merges the patch into the matching step. It then saves the updated steps array through update.

symbols.does: Moves a step from one index to another.
symbols.description: Checks target bounds, splices the selected step out, inserts it at the requested index, saves the reordered steps, and makes the moved step active.

symbols.does: Removes a step while keeping at least one step in the wizard.
symbols.description: Blocks removal when only one step remains and shows a toast. Otherwise it filters out the step and adjusts the active index to a valid remaining position.

symbols.does: Appends a new step of a selected kind.
symbols.description: Creates a step with a timestamp/random id, default title from STEP_KINDS, empty body, and kind-specific default fields or options. It saves the appended steps array and selects the new step.

symbols.does: Renders the right-side editor for a single wizard step.
symbols.description: Provides controls for step kind, title, body, form fields, choice options, choice branching, and Vi refinement. It also exposes an alternate-generation button when an onAlternate callback is provided.

symbols.does: Patches one field in the current step.
symbols.description: Maps over step.fields and shallow-merges the patch into the field at the requested index. It emits the changed fields array through onChange.

symbols.does: Deletes one field from the current step.
symbols.description: Filters step.fields by index and sends the remaining fields through onChange. It is used by field-row remove buttons in form editing.

symbols.does: Adds a default text field to the current step.
symbols.description: Appends a field object with label "Field" and kind "text" to the existing fields array, using an empty array fallback. It emits the new fields list through onChange.

symbols.does: Updates one choice option string.
symbols.description: Maps over step.options and replaces the option at the requested index with the supplied value. It emits the changed options array through onChange.

symbols.does: Removes one choice option.
symbols.description: Filters step.options by index and sends the remaining options through onChange. It is used in the choice option editor.

symbols.does: Adds a numbered default choice option.
symbols.description: Appends "Option N" to the current options array, where N is derived from the current option count. It uses an empty fallback array when options are absent.

symbols.does: Renders an inline Vi prompt for revising the active step.
symbols.description: Maintains local prompt text and busy state. It asks Vi for a revised step JSON object and applies returned title, body, fields, and options while preserving existing values when fields are absent.

symbols.does: Runs the step refinement request.
symbols.description: Validates non-empty input and busy state, builds a prompt containing the current step and user request, calls window.claude.complete, parses JSON from the response, applies recognized step fields, and reports success or failure through toasts.

symbols.does: Renders the wizard preview in the selected layout.
symbols.description: Chooses the active step from the document and renders sidebar, progress, or card-stack layout. It passes prototype state and navigation callbacks into StepBody and allows jumping between steps in prototype mode.

symbols.does: Renders the contents and navigation controls for a wizard step.
symbols.description: Displays step heading, body text, and kind-specific UI for capture, form, choice, review, or celebrate steps. It reads and writes prototype state by step id and shows Back/Continue/Finish actions.

symbols.does: Writes a field value into prototype state for the current step.
symbols.description: No-ops outside prototype mode. In prototype mode it merges the new field value into the current step's state bucket while preserving other stored values.

symbols.does: Renders a field input based on a field schema.
symbols.description: Handles choice fields as selects, file fields as a visual filebox, number fields as numeric inputs, and all other fields as text inputs. Inputs are disabled when not in prototype mode except the filebox, which is visual-only.

symbols.does: Draws a small SVG glyph for a named shape.
symbols.description: Supports hexagon, diamond, and circle using accent color CSS variables. The hexagon points are computed from the requested size, while diamond and circle use fixed SVG primitives derived from size.

symbols.does: Renders export actions for the current wizard document.
symbols.description: Computes generated JSX with useMemo and exposes buttons for copying JSX, downloading a .jsx file, and printing to PDF. It stops click propagation so the menu can stay open while interacting with it.

symbols.does: Downloads the generated wizard JSX as a file.
symbols.description: Creates a text Blob from the generated code, creates an object URL, clicks a temporary anchor with a slugified filename, revokes the URL, and closes the export menu.

symbols.does: Starts browser printing and closes the export menu.
symbols.description: Calls window.print for print-to-PDF workflows. It then invokes the optional onClose callback.

symbols.does: Copies generated JSX to the clipboard.
symbols.description: Uses navigator.clipboard.writeText when available and shows a toast saying the JSX was copied. It does not close the export menu.

symbols.does: Generates a standalone React component string for the wizard.
symbols.description: Serializes doc.steps into a STEPS constant and returns JSX source with local idx/state handling, a sidebar step list, field rendering, choice rendering, and navigation buttons. The generated component name is based on pascalCase(doc.title).

symbols.does: Represents the generated default React component inside the wizardToJSX template string.
symbols.description: The template component initializes idx and state, derives the active step, updates field state by step id, renders the sidebar and main content, and advances or backs up through STEPS. Its actual function name is interpolated from the document title.

symbols.does: Updates generated-component state for one field on the active step.
symbols.description: This function exists inside the JSX string emitted by wizardToJSX. It merges a field value into the active step's nested state object using the generated component's setState callback.

symbols.does: Converts text into a PascalCase identifier fragment.
symbols.description: Removes non-alphanumeric separators, splits into words, title-cases each word, and joins them. It falls back to "Wizard" when no usable text remains.

symbols.does: Converts text into a lowercase dash-separated slug.
symbols.description: Lowercases the input, replaces non-alphanumeric runs with hyphens, trims leading and trailing hyphens, and falls back to "wizard" for empty results.
